commit_base: 74a53a0 + cherry-picks (Stage 3-5 + epoch nonce + SEMPER_NUM_NODES + Theme-D ring)
platform: Docker QEMU 2-node, qemu64+rdrand+fsgsbase+xsave+xsaveopt, e1000 socket networking

spanning_exchange_walltime.definition: Cross-kernel OBTAIN through session at sel 300 (true round-trip K0->DTUBridge->net->K1->VPE1->reply). VPE0-side rdtsc walltime.

spanning_revoke_walltime.definition: REVOKE of an OBTAINed cap (cap has remote parent on peer, no remote children — local-only revoke per Hille tier-1).
spanning_revoke_walltime.caveat: Does NOT exercise cross-kernel REVOKE — that requires revoking a cap WITH remote children (e.g. the session itself, as in Test 15b). Future bench iteration should revoke the session cap per iteration.

spanning_chain_revoke_10_walltime.caveat: Chain root is the OBTAINed cap with remote parent. Revoke walks local chain only — doesn't appear to scale with depth, suggesting either chain is not actually built (delegate semantics) or chain walk is faster than measurement noise.

delta_factor_vs_gem5.exchange: 47.8M / 6.5K = ~7400x (round trip dominates: emulated E1000 + UDP + ChaCha20-Poly1305 vs lossless instant gem5 NoC)
delta_factor_vs_gem5.revoke: 154K / 1.99K = ~77x (local revoke; explained by QEMU TCG syscall overhead vs gem5)
delta_factor_vs_gem5.chain_100: 135K / 200K = 0.67x (LOWER than gem5 — see caveat: bench likely not exercising chain walk correctly)
